question: Extend the `Animal` and `Dog` classes. Make the `name` member in `Animal` `protected`. Demonstrate how `Dog` can access and modify `name` directly, while an outside function cannot.

test_cases.expected:
Animal name: Buddy
Dog changed name to: Rex
Animal name: Rex
Outside function attempting to access name (should fail if uncommented):

driver: int main() { Dog myDog("Buddy"); myDog.displayAnimalName(); myDog.changeName("Rex"); myDog.displayAnimalName(); outsideFunction(myDog); return 0; }
errors: Missing semicolon after class definition of 'Animal'. This will cause a compilation error.
